originalTitle: 'This tree was planted by my ancestor hundreds of years ago and my family settled here'
title: A Super Old Tree Tells a Family's Story!
content:
Hello, Super Friends from Africa!

Hi everyone! It's your kid-friendly news reporter here, bringing you an amazing story from our wonderful continent, Africa! Today, we are going on a special adventure to a country called Ghana. Ghana is a beautiful country in West Africa, full of sunshine, friendly people, and amazing stories. Are you ready for a really cool tale about a tree and a family?

Meet Our Giant Tree Friend!

Imagine a tree. Not just any tree, but a super, super old tree! This tree has been alive for so, so many years. It's been alive for longer than your grandparents, longer than your great-grandparents, and even longer than their great-grandparents! Wow! This tree is like a very wise old friend who has seen many, many things. Guess how old it is? People say it was planted way back in the 13th Century! That's almost 800 years ago! Can you imagine? This tree was just a tiny seed when very few people lived in the world as we know it today. It has grown big and strong, reaching high into the sky, watching over generations of people.

Where is This Special Tree?

This incredible tree lives in a special place in Ghana. It's in a town right next to the big, blue ocean. What do you call towns next to the ocean? That's right, a fishing town! In this town, people go out in their boats to catch fish for their families and to sell in the market. The air smells salty and fresh, and you can always hear the sound of the waves. This tree stands tall and proud, like a giant landmark. It gives cool shade on sunny days, and maybe little birds like to build nests in its big branches. It has been a part of the town for longer than anyone can remember.

A Family's Amazing Story

Now, here's where the story gets even more exciting! There is a special family in this fishing town. They have an amazing secret. They know that this super old tree was planted by their ancestor! Do you know what an ancestor is? It's like a very, very old family member who lived a long, long, long time ago. Like your great-great-great-grandparent, but even further back! This family says that their first ancestor came to this place and planted this tree. It was like putting down their first ever root in the soil, just like the tree did. When the tree grew, their family grew too, and they made this town their home.

What is "Oral History"?

How do they know this story? Did someone write it down in a book? Not always! This family knows their story through something called "oral history." Oral history means stories that are told from person to person, from generation to generation. It's like your grandmother telling you a story about when she was little, and she heard it from her grandmother, and so on. Think about it like this: A grandpa tells his child, and then that child grows up and tells their child, and that child tells their child. For hundreds of years, this family has kept the story of the tree alive just by telling it. It's a special way to remember important things from the past. Did you know? Many African cultures have rich oral histories. It's how important lessons, traditions, and family stories are passed down without needing to write them in books!

How Old is "13th Century"?

Let's talk about the "13th Century" again. That's a very long time ago! If we are in the 21st Century now, the 13th Century was eight hundred years ago. Imagine eight hundred birthdays for the tree! That means the tree was planted around the year 1200 something. It's so old that many countries and cities that we know today didn't even exist then! When this tree was a baby, kings and queens might have been riding horses and living in castles far away. It's a true ancient wonder right here in Africa!

Why is This Tree So Important?

This tree is more than just a plant; it's like a living history book for the family. It helps them remember where they came from. It reminds them of their first ancestor and how their family started in this special fishing town. It connects them to the past in a very real way. It helps them feel strong and proud of their roots. Just like a tree has strong roots that hold it to the ground, this story gives the family strong roots to their past. It helps them understand who they are and where their family journey began. Did you know? Trees give us many things! They give us fresh air to breathe, yummy fruits, wood for building, and beautiful shade to rest under. This tree gives even more: a family's history!

What Can We Learn?

This amazing story from Ghana teaches us so much! It teaches us that nature, like trees, can hold incredible stories. It teaches us about the power of family stories and how important it is to listen to our elders. Maybe you have special family stories too! Ask your grandparents or parents about your family's history. You might discover something wonderful! It also shows us that history isn't just in books. Sometimes, it's in the land, in a special tree, or in the stories we share. So, next time you see a big, old tree, remember the amazing tree in Ghana that tells a family's ancient tale. It makes us think about our own roots and where we come from. What a fantastic story from our amazing continent, Africa!

Bye for now!

That's all for today, my super news friends! Keep exploring, keep listening, and keep discovering the magic around you! See you next time for more exciting news from Africa!
summary: A family in Ghana traces their roots back hundreds of years to a very old tree planted by their ancestor in a fishing town, keeping their history alive through oral stories.
whyMatters: This story matters because it helps people remember where their family started and how important their ancestors are. It's like a special, living history book that connects them to their past and helps them understand who they are.

content: The former leader has not responded to US accusations that he is aiming to destabilise the government.

originalTitle: China scraps tariffs for all but one African nation
title: Super News! China Helps Africa's Businesses!
content:
Hello, my amazing friends across Africa! It's your friendly news reporter, here to share some super exciting news that's going to make a big difference for our continent! Are you ready? Let's go!

A Big Wave Hello!

Imagine you have a friend who lives very far away, in a country called China. You want to send them some delicious mangoes from your garden, or maybe a beautiful hand-carved toy made by someone in your village. But sometimes, when things travel from one country to another, there's an extra charge, like a special fee you have to pay. This extra charge is called a "tariff." It's like paying a small toll fee on a bridge, but for goods!

What Are Tariffs Anyway?

A tariff is like a little bit of extra money that a country asks for when goods from another country come into their shops. So, if your mangoes went to China, someone there might have to pay a little extra money because they came from Africa. This extra money makes the mangoes a little bit more expensive for the people in China to buy. **Did you know?** Countries use tariffs for different reasons, sometimes to make things from their own country cheaper than things from other countries, so people buy local!

No More Extra Money! Hooray!

Guess what? Our friend China has just made a super kind announcement! They said, "For almost all African countries, you don't have to pay that extra money anymore when you send your goods to China!" Isn't that wonderful? It means that things like our yummy fruits, our amazing coffee, our beautiful crafts, and many other things from Africa can now be sold in China without that extra charge.

Why Is This So Good?

When there are no tariffs, our African products become cheaper for people in China to buy. And when things are cheaper, more people want to buy them! This means that our farmers, our artists, and our factories in Africa can sell much more of what they make. More sales mean more money for our communities!

Let's Talk About Trade!

When countries buy and sell things to each other, it's called "trade." It's like when you swap your snack with a friend – you both get something new and yummy! Now, with no tariffs, trade between Africa and China will be much easier and bigger. Our African countries can send more of their special goods to China, and China can send things they make to us too. It's a win-win! **Did you know?** Africa is a huge continent with many different countries, each making unique and wonderful things! From delicious cocoa beans in Ghana to colorful textiles in Nigeria, Africa has so much to offer the world!

More Money for Our People!

Imagine a farmer who grows the best coffee beans. Before, when he sent his coffee to China, there was an extra tariff. Now, without that tariff, his coffee is more affordable, and more people in China will want to drink it. This means the farmer can sell more coffee, earn more money, and maybe buy new tools for his farm, or send his children to school!

More Jobs and Brighter Futures!

When businesses sell more things, they often need more people to help them. This means more jobs for our moms, dads, brothers, and sisters! More jobs mean families have more money for food, clothes, and a good home. It also means our countries can use this extra money to build better schools, better hospitals, and better roads. Isn't that amazing?

What Kinds of Things Can Africa Send?

Africa grows so many delicious foods! Think about juicy mangoes, sweet pineapples, rich coffee, and flavorful spices. We also make beautiful handcrafted items, like woven baskets, wooden carvings, and colorful fabrics. These are just some of the wonderful things that can now travel to China without that extra fee, making them more popular! **Did you know?** Many countries in Africa are known for their delicious fruits like bananas, oranges, and avocados. Imagine these yummy treats being enjoyed by more people far away!

A Friend Helping Friends

China is showing that they want to be a very good friend to Africa. By removing these tariffs, they are saying, "We want to help you grow and be successful!" This kind act helps build stronger bonds and friendship between our continent and China.

Is It Fair for Everyone?

Sometimes, when something big happens, some countries might benefit a little bit more than others. Like when you share a big cake, some friends might get a slightly bigger slice, but everyone still gets some cake! This new rule is a great step forward, and it helps almost all African countries. The important thing is that it opens up so many new doors for everyone to sell more and grow! **Did you know?** There are 54 countries in Africa! Each one is special and important. This new rule from China helps 53 of them directly!

What This Means for YOU!

Even if you are a young student, this news is important for you! When our countries do well, our communities do well. This means better opportunities for everyone. It means more resources for your schools, more books to read, and perhaps even new playgrounds! It helps create a brighter, stronger future for all of us in Africa. So, let's celebrate this wonderful news! China is making it easier for Africa to share its amazing products with the world, helping our farmers, businesses, and communities thrive. It’s a truly exciting time for Africa!
summary: China has decided to remove special extra fees, called tariffs, on almost all goods coming from Africa, making it easier and cheaper for African businesses to sell their products in China and helping Africa grow.
whyMatters: This is super important because it means African farmers and businesses can sell more things, earn more money, create more jobs, and help build better schools and hospitals for everyone in our communities.
